title: Grafana LDAP and OAuth Team Sync Failure Events
dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:log` auth client (`auth.client_error`, LDAP, OAuth), `grafana:audit` sync failures
spl:
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:log" (ldap OR oauth OR oidc OR "team sync")
| search "error" OR "failed" OR "invalid_grant" OR "unable to authenticate" OR "connection reset"
| stats count, latest(_time) as t, values(logger) as loggers by auth_provider, host
| where count >= 10
| sort -count
description: Identity sync failures strand new hires without dashboard access and can leave stale group memberships in regulated environments. Centralizing LDAP/OAuth errors accelerates IdP cutovers and surfaces certificate or clock skew issues.
value: Prevents productivity loss after directory changes and tightens access consistency across HA Grafana nodes.
implementation: Tag `auth_provider` (`ldap`, `azuread`, `okta`). Correlate with IdP health dashboards. Alert on sustained `count` growth versus 7-day baseline, not single blips.
visualization: Timechart by provider, table of error signatures, link to IdP incident tickets.
grandmaExplanation: We watch our primary metrics and dashboard platform for query errors, slow views, and changes that could break the screens teams use every day.
knownFalsePositives: Noisy periods after retention, target, or scrape changes. We compare with the Grafana or Prometheus view for the same minutes.
